title: Same-note guard returns synthetic NewSession instead of no-op per REQ-EPNS-005
description: REQ-EPNS-005 states: 'the system SHALL treat the selection as a no-op: no flush, no new session creation'. The acceptance criteria explicitly say 'EditingSessionState is unchanged' and 'classifyCurrentSession is NOT called'. However, pipeline.ts lines 77-86 construct and return a `NewSession` object (with `ok: true, value: { kind: 'NewSession', ... }`) on the same-note path. This is semantically incorrect: the caller receives a result indistinguishable from a successful note switch, when the spec requires no session creation. A caller relying on the return type to decide whether to update EditingSessionState would incorrectly transition state. The return type of the pipeline function (`Result<NewSession, SwitchError>`) has no representation for 'no-op / same-note'. This is a design gap.
recommendation: Introduce a discriminated return type that distinguishes 'same-note no-op' from 'new session created'. For example: `Result<NewSession | SameNoteNoOp, SwitchError>` where `SameNoteNoOp = { kind: 'SameNoteNoOp' }`. Alternatively, change the return to a three-variant result. The caller must not treat same-note as a new session.
